name: Peñas Crecientes desde Pinilla del Valle – circular Parque Nacional de Guadarrama
region_intro: Mountain biking around Pinilla Del Valle offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, featuring high mountains, extensive pine and oak forests, and the scenic Lozoya Valley. The region is characterized by varied elevation profiles, from challenging mountain ascents to more gentle paths along the Pinilla Reservoir. Riders can explore a rich natural environment shaped by the Lozoya River and its tributaries, providing a mix of forest trails and open vistas. This area is a prime destination for mountain biking enthusiasts seeking natural landscapes in the Community of Madrid. Best mountain bike trails around Pinilla Del Valle The most popular mountain bike trail is Peñas Crecientes desde Pinilla del Valle – circular Parque Nacional de Guadarrama, a difficult 42.5 miles (68.4 km) trail that takes 7 hours 16 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through the national park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Matafrailes Bridge – Canto Bridge of Canencia loop from Alameda del Valle, a difficult 34.3 miles (55.2 km) path. This trail winds through forested areas and crosses notable bridges. Local mountain bikers also love the Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint – Matafrailes Bridge loop from Pinilla del Valle, a 28.4 miles (45.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including reservoir views, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Pinilla Del Valle is defined by high mountain ecosystems, extensive forests, and the scenic Pinilla Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging ascents. The routes in Pinilla Del Valle are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pinilla Del Valle's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Pinilla Del Valle?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails around Pinilla Del Valle, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These routes traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, including high mountains, extensive forests, and the scenic Lozoya Valley.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Pinilla Del Valle?
A: Mountain biking in Pinilla Del Valle features varied terrain, from challenging ascents in high mountain ecosystems to more gentle paths along the Pinilla Reservoir. You'll encounter extensive pine and oak forests, open vistas, and trails shaped by the Lozoya River and its tributaries. The region offers a mix of technical sections and flowing singletrack.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Pinilla Del Valle?
A: Yes, Pinilla Del Valle offers several routes suitable for families. While many trails can be challenging, there are 14 easy and 28 moderate routes that provide a less strenuous experience, often featuring scenic views without extreme elevation changes. These trails are great for enjoying the natural environment with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Pinilla Del Valle?
A: Pinilla del Valle is located within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, which has specific regulations regarding dogs. While many areas are dog-friendly, it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always check local signage and park regulations before heading out.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore the diverse natural environment of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Highlights include the scenic Pinilla Reservoir, the extensive Mojonavalle Forest, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nevero Peak&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter ancient trees like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3019093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oaks of the Parada del Rey&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yew of the Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region around Pinilla Del Valle is home to several beautiful waterfalls. While mountain biking, you might find trails that lead near or offer views of cascades such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/851377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purgatory Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2685066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de la Vejiga&lt;/a&gt;. These natural attractions provide excellent spots for a break.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Pinilla Del Valle?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Pinilla Del Valle, particularly near trailheads and in the village itself. As the area is popular for outdoor activities, designated parking areas are often provided. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Pinilla Del Valle?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Pinilla Del Valle is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher parts of the Sierra de Guadarrama, making some trails inaccessible or requiring specialized equipment.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Pinilla Del Valle are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example of a popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31551055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint – Matafrailes Bridge loop from Pinilla del Valle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied landscapes and reservoir views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Pinilla Del Valle?
A: The mountain bike trails in Pinilla Del Valle are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Can I reach the mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Pinilla Del Valle are available, primarily by bus from Madrid. While buses can get you to the village, transporting bikes may have restrictions or require specific services. It's recommended to check with the local transport providers for their bike carriage policies before planning your trip.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike routes for beginners?
A: Yes, Pinilla Del Valle offers 14 routes classified as easy, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These trails typically have less elevation gain and smoother surfaces, allowing new riders to enjoy the scenery without excessive challenge.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike route in Pinilla Del Valle?
A: One of the longest and most challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e960747515/penas-crecientes-desde-pinilla-del-valle-circular-parque-nacional-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Crecientes desde Pinilla del Valle – circular Parque Nacional de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult trail spans over 68 kilometers and involves significant elevation gain, offering an epic adventure for experienced mountain bikers.
tips: The climb to the top of Peñas Crecientes (2,204 masl) can be physically demanding, but the views will be a great reward for the effort. There are several paths to get there, although according to the regulations of the Guadarrama National Park, it is forbidden to ride a bike in some of them, plan the route well to avoid any problem.
Very basic mountain refuge, but perfect in the event of a storm, with a very mighty stream next to it.
Wonderful bike path around the reservoir.
